heading: Additional measures at donor ports and energy transfer ports
label: 2238c Additional measures at donor ports and energy transfer ports
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Water Resources Reform and Development Act of 2014, and not as part of the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Cargo container
children.label: 1 Cargo container
children.text: The term “cargo container” means a cargo container that is 1 Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit.

children.heading: Discretionary cargo
children.label: 2 Discretionary cargo
children.text: The term “discretionary cargo” means maritime cargo for which the United States port of unlading is different than the United States port of entry.

children.label: i
children.text: that is subject to the harbor maintenance fee under section 24.24 of title 19, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation);

children.label: ii
children.text: at which the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes collected (including the estimated taxes related to domestic cargo and cruise passengers) comprise not less than $15,000,000 annually of the total funding of the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund on an average annual basis for the previous 3 fiscal years;

children.label: iii
children.text: that received less than 25 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes collected (including the estimated taxes related to domestic cargo and cruise passengers) at that port in the previous 3 fiscal years; and

children.label: iv
children.text: that is located in a State in which more than 2,000,000 cargo containers were unloaded from or loaded on to vessels on an average annual basis for the previous 3 fiscal years.

children.heading: Calculation
children.label: B Calculation
children.text: For the purpose of calculating the percentage described in subparagraph (A)(iii), payments described under subsection (c)(1) shall not be included.

children.label: A
children.text: petroleum products;

children.label: B
children.text: natural gas;

children.label: C
children.text: coal;

children.label: D
children.text: wind and solar energy components; and

children.label: E
children.text: biofuels.

children.label: A
children.text: that is subject to the harbor maintenance fee under section 24.24 of title 19, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation); and

children.label: i
children.text: at which energy commodities comprised greater than 25 percent of all commercial activity by tonnage on an average annual basis for the previous 3 fiscal years; and

children.label: ii
children.text: through which more than 40,000,000 tons of cargo were transported on an average annual basis for the previous 3 fiscal years.

children.heading: Expanded uses
children.label: 6 Expanded uses
children.text: The term “expanded uses” has the meaning given the term in .section 2238(f) of this title

children.heading: Harbor maintenance tax
children.label: 7 Harbor maintenance tax
children.text: The term “harbor maintenance tax” has the meaning given the term in .section 2238(f) of this title

children.heading: Harbor maintenance trust fund
children.label: 8 Harbor maintenance trust fund
children.text: The term “Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund” means the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund established by .section 9505 of title 26

children.label: A
children.text: that is subject to the harbor maintenance fee under section 24.24 of title 19, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation);

children.label: B
children.text: at which the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes collected (including the estimated taxes related to domestic cargo and cruise passengers) comprise annually more than $5,000,000 but less than $15,000,000 of the total funding of the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund on an average annual basis for the previous 3 fiscal years;

children.label: C
children.text: that received less than 25 percent of the total amount of harbor maintenance taxes collected (including the estimated taxes related to domestic cargo and cruise passengers) at that port in the previous 3 fiscal years; and

children.label: D
children.text: that is located in a State in which more than 2,000,000 cargo containers were unloaded from or loaded onto vessels on an average annual basis for the previous 3 fiscal years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary may provide to donor ports, medium-sized donor ports, and energy transfer ports amounts in accordance with this section.

children.label: A
children.text: for energy transfer ports shall be divided equally among all States with an energy transfer port;

children.label: B
children.text: shall be made available to a port as either a donor port, medium-sized donor port, or an energy transfer port, and no port may receive amounts from more than 1 designation; and

children.label: i
children.text: 50 percent of the funds shall be equally divided between the eligible donor ports as authorized by this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 50 percent of the funds shall be divided between the eligible donor ports and eligible medium-sized donor ports based on the percentage of the total harbor maintenance tax revenues generated at each eligible donor port and medium-sized donor port.
to provide payments to importers entering cargo through that port, as calculated by the Secretary according to the value of discretionary cargo;
for expanded uses; or
for environmental remediation related to dredging berths and Federal navigation channels.

children.label: A
children.text: shipped through the port; and

children.label: B
children.text: most at risk of diversion to seaports outside of the United States.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 2 Requirement
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with a port electing to provide payments under subsection (c), shall determine the top importers at the port, as ranked by the value of discretionary cargo, and payments shall be limited to those top importers.

children.label: A
children.text: $56,000,000 for fiscal year 2023;

children.label: B
children.text: $58,000,000 for fiscal year 2024;

children.label: C
children.text: $60,000,000 for fiscal year 2025;

children.label: D
children.text: $62,000,000 for fiscal year 2026;

children.label: E
children.text: $64,000,000 for fiscal year 2027;

children.label: F
children.text: $66,000,000 for fiscal year 2028;

children.label: G
children.text: $68,000,000 for fiscal year 2029; and

children.label: H
children.text: $70,000,000 for fiscal year 2030.

children.heading: Division between donor ports, medium-sized donor ports, and energy transfer ports
children.label: 2 Division between donor ports, medium-sized donor ports, and energy transfer ports
A
children.text: donor ports and medium-sized donor ports; and

children.label: B
children.text: energy transfer ports.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: f Savings clause
children.text: Nothing in this section waives any statutory requirement related to the transportation of merchandise as authorized under chapter 551 of title 46.
